title: Meibomia scalpe
taxonomy: Meibomia scalpe is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It is native to the southeastern United States and belongs to the genus Meibomia. The genus Meibomia is a group of flowering plants that are often found in woodland areas and are known for their clusters of small white flowers. Meibomia scalpe is a perennial herb that produces clusters of small white flowers with purple or pink markings. The plant is often found growing in shaded areas with rich soil.

intro: Meibomia scalpe, also known as Coreopsis scalpe, is a perennial plant species native to North America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which make it beneficial for wound healing, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Meibomia scalpe include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
